issue_title: ES|QL TS: _timeseries column returns non-JSON bytes
issue_body:
### Elasticsearch Version

9.4

### Installed Plugins

_No response_

### Java Version

_bundled_

### OS Version

any

### Problem Description

The _timeseries keyword column produced by the ES|QL TS command is documented to contain a JSON-encoded object with the dimension key/value pairs identifying each group.

In practice, TimeSeriesMetadataFieldBlockLoader emits _timeseries as the raw bytes of _source in whatever XContent format _source was stored in. When the index uses index.mapping.source.mode: stored and the documents were indexed with a non-JSON XContent (e.g. CBOR — as the Prometheus remote-write handler does), the value rendered to the user is uninterpretable string.

License: enterprise

### Steps to Reproduce

1. Send some metrics through the Prometheus remote-write endpoint, which writes documents using CBOR (PrometheusRemoteWriteTransportAction#buildIndexRequest calls XContentFactory.cborBuilder(...)).
2. Run an ES|QL TS query that triggers the implicit "group by all dimensions" behavior:
```
TS metrics-*.prometheus-*
| WHERE @timestamp > NOW() - 15m
| STATS LAST_OVER_TIME(go_gc_cleanups_executed_cleanups_total)
```

### Expected behavior
The _timeseries value is a UTF-8 JSON string, e.g.:
```
{"labels":{"__name__":"go_gc_cleanups_executed_cleanups_total","instance":"localhost:9090","job":"prometheus"}}
```

### Actual behavior
The _timeseries value is the raw CBOR _source bytes interpreted as a string:
```
{
  "columns": [
    { "name": "LAST_OVER_TIME(go_gc_cleanups_executed_cleanups_total)", "type": "double" },
    { "name": "_timeseries", "type": "keyword" }
  ],
  "values": [
    [ 0, "\ufffdflabels\ufffdh__name__x&go_gc_cleanups_executed_cleanups_totalhinstancenlocalhost:9090cjobjprometheus\ufffd\ufffd" ]
  ]
}
```

Decoding the bytes confirms it is the synthetic CBOR encoding of `{"labels":{"__name__":"go_gc_cleanups_executed_cleanups_total","instance":"localhost:9090","job":"prometheus"}}`:

Byte(s) | CBOR meaning
-- | --
0xa1 | map of 1 entry
0x66 "labels" | key "labels"
0xa3 | inner map of 3 entries
0x68 "__name__" | key "__name__"
0x78 0x26 "go_gc_..." | text-string of length 38
... | instance, localhost:9090, job, prometheus
0xff 0xff | break markers

### Logs (if relevant)

_No response_
commit_message:
Normalize `_timeseries` to JSON for stored source (#148716)

The `_timeseries` keyword column is documented to contain a JSON
object with the dimension key/value pairs identifying each group,
but `TimeSeriesMetadataFieldBlockLoader#read` was streaming the raw
`_source` bytes into the block builder without checking the
encoding.

With stored source, the default for time-series indices on
non-Enterprise licenses, and the path the Prometheus remote-write
endpoint uses. Those bytes are CBOR (built via
`XContentFactory.cborBuilder`), so the column came out as binary
garbage in ES|QL queries against `metrics-*.prometheus-*`.

Normalize to JSON whenever the underlying `XContentType` is not
already JSON. When it is JSON we return the bytes unchanged to
avoid a parser/builder round-trip on the hot read path.

commit_message:
Remove flaky agent assertion in nodes.stats yaml test (#148546)

The clients.0.agent field can be null: the clients array order is
non-deterministic and a tracked channel that closes before any
request reaches update() ends up at index 0 with all request fields
unset. Agent extraction is covered by HttpClientStatsTrackerTests.

Closes #148430

issue_title: ESQL: USER_AGENT, REGISTERED_DOMAIN, URI_PARTS (CompoundOutputEval commands) lead to incorrectly optimized plans
issue_body:
### Description

This came out from generative testing. The original query was much more complex, a minimal reproducer below:

```
  ROW a = 1, name_str = "a b"
  | RENAME a AS id_int
  | SORT id_int
  | MV_EXPAND name_str
  | REGISTERED_DOMAIN g = "www.example.com"
  | STATS c = count(*) BY g.domain
```

leads to 

```
Plan [OrderBy[[Order[a{r}#2153,ASC,LAST]]]] optimized incorrectly due to missing references [a{r}#2153]",
```

Generating commands tested in the slot between MV_EXPAND and STATS:

**Reproduce the bug:**

  - REGISTERED_DOMAIN
  - URI_PARTS
  - USER_AGENT

**Do NOT reproduce**:
  - ENRICH
  - RERANK
  - DISSECT
  - GROK
  - EVAL
  - COMPLETION  and RERANK untested but likely do not repro

The triggering commands all share the "scalar-function-style generating command that emits multiple dotted-name attributes from a single constant input" shape; that shape goes through a planner path the others avoid.

commit_message:
ES|QL|DS: drop static-flag check from DatasetRewriter (#148859)

DatasetRewriter.rewrite() no longer reads
ESQL_EXTERNAL_DATASOURCES_FEATURE_FLAG.isEnabled() itself. The
rewriter's other early-exits (projectMetadata == null,
datasets.isEmpty()) cover the no-op path; the feature flag still
gates the CRUD layer that puts datasets into cluster state, so when
the flag is off there are no datasets and the rewriter bails on
datasets.isEmpty() as it always did.

DatasetRewriterTests had an @Before assumeTrue(flag.isEnabled())
guard plus a testFeatureFlagOffLeavesPlanUnchanged for the off-path.
In release-tests CI the @Before observed the flag as on (test ran)
while the rewriter's own check inside the body observed it as off
(rewriter no-op'd), failing 20 of 29 tests. Removing the rewriter's
static-flag read breaks the test class's dependency on the static.

Closes #148666

commit_message:
Fix SharedBlobCacheWarmingServiceTests.testIdLookupPreWarming test (#148826)

The test assertion is validating whether only the region containing the .tim file footer, assuming the footer always fits a single region.

Unfortunately, in some cases the footer spans two regions (last and second last) - e.g. 4 first bytes in the last region, but 12 bytes are in the second last.

This is a valid case (not an issue), so we should just accept that.

commit_message:
Fix FsBlobContainer.moveBlobAtomic fallback on CIFS filesystems (#148777)

The Files.createLink() in java states that in case of no hard-link support, the UnsupportedOperationException is thrown.
Unfortunately, in reality the UnixFileSystemProvider may throw a generic FileSystemException if a native method call fails for this very reason.

To handle this case, we're going to catch raw FileSystemException exceptions and call a safe fall-back that does not use hard links. If there is a different reason of this operation throwing exception, the fallback should handle this properly.

issue_title: [One workflow] Workflows data stream mapping definition drift
issue_body:
## Summary

Workflows-related **system data streams** are registered in `es` via `SystemDataStreamDescriptor`, with composable templates shipped in [this repository](https://github.com/elastic/elasticsearch/blob/ebcf8934a2bfc514d1afe3fdc286a3434251f25c/modules/kibana/src/main/resources/org/elasticsearch/kibana/workflows-events.json#L21). Those templates currently set `"dynamic": true` and define only a minimal `properties` block.

Separately, [kibana codebase](https://github.com/elastic/kibana/blob/fa2ad7a7aeaf0557bbe9dd5b5339e6174105fd05/src/platform/plugins/shared/workflows_execution_engine/server/trigger_events/event_logs/trigger_events_data_stream.ts#L31) defines mappings for the same logical documents (e.g. trigger / execution event fields) with `dynamic: false` and explicit `properties`.

That is **two sources of truth** for one schema. Contributors can believe the strict kibana mapping is what elasticsearch enforces, while the cluster may still **auto-map new fields** per the bundled template. The **effective** mapping is whatever elasticsearch has persisted on the data stream / backing indices **not** what the TypeScript object says unless it is proven to be applied identically at create time.

### Why this is a bug

1. **Contradictory defaults**: `dynamic: true` vs `dynamic: false` is not cosmetic; it changes whether unknown document keys become mapped fields.
2. **No single owner**: Risk of Kibana-only schema changes never reflected in `es` templates (or the reverse), and silent divergence.
3. **Wrong mental model**: Engineers may assume kibana's mapping is authoritative for the cluster without checking **`GET <data_stream>/_mapping`** on a real deployment.

## Expected behavior

One agreed **canonical** mapping for each workflows system data stream, with:

- The same **`dynamic`** policy everywhere docs claim the schema lives, and
- The same **explicit `properties`** for fields that must be queryable/filterable, and
- A deliberate **version bump** in this repo when the template changes.


## Fix

**Elasticsearch as source of truth** 
Change the JSON composable templates to match Kibana’s intended schema (`dynamic: false`, full `properties` list). Bump `WORKFLOWS_EVENTS_MAPPINGS_VERSION` and/or `WORKFLOWS_EXECUTION_LOGS_MAPPINGS_VERSION` in `KibanaPlugin.java`. In Kibana, **remove or narrow** duplicate mapping payloads to types-only / comments pointing at this repo.

issue_title: AssertionError / silent inner-hits loss when combining field collapse with nested query inner_hits
issue_body:
### Elasticsearch Version

v9.5

### Installed Plugins

_No response_

### Java Version

_bundled_

### OS Version

all

### Problem Description

When a search request uses field collapsing with inner_hits and simultaneously has a nested query with its own inner_hits, the two phases that attach inner hits to a SearchHit collide. The result is:

  - With -ea (assertions on): java.lang.AssertionError in a search thread → ElasticsearchUncaughtExceptionHandler kills the node mid-request (curl exit 52).
  - Without -ea (production default): The assertion is silently skipped. ExpandSearchPhase overwrites the nested-query inner hits with the collapse inner hits, so the nested inner_hits entries disappear from the response without any error.

Stack Trace

  [ERROR][o.e.b.ElasticsearchUncaughtExceptionHandler] fatal error in thread
    [elasticsearch[...][search][T#14]], exiting
  java.lang.AssertionError: null
      at org.elasticsearch.search.SearchHit.setInnerHits(SearchHit.java:692)
      at org.elasticsearch.action.search.ExpandSearchPhase.lambda$doRun$0(ExpandSearchPhase.java:132)
      at org.elasticsearch.action.ActionListener$2.onResponse(ActionListener.java:280)
      ...
      at org.elasticsearch.action.search.FetchSearchPhase$1.doRun(FetchSearchPhase.java:83)
  


### Steps to Reproduce

Minimal Repro Shape

  POST /my-index/_search
  {
    "_source": true,
    "collapse": {
      "field": "group_id",
      "inner_hits": { "name": "collapse_hits", "size": 1 }
    },
    "query": {
      "nested": {
        "path": "items",
        "inner_hits": { "name": "nested_hits", "size": 1 },
        "query": { "match_all": {} }
      }
    }
  }


### Logs (if relevant)

_No response_
commit_message:
Fix inner hits overwrite when collapse and nested query both set inner hits (#148915)

SearchHit.setInnerHits and withInnerHits asserted this.innerHits == null, causing an AssertionError (with -ea) or silent data loss (without) when a search combined field collapsing with inner_hits and a nested query that also had inner_hits. Both InnerHitsPhase and ExpandSearchPhase call setInnerHits on the same hit; their key sets are always disjoint, so the fix merges the maps instead of overwriting.

Added tests to guard against regression.

Closes #148905.
